Name: TRIPOD+AI
LongName: TRIPOD+AI statement: updated guidance for reporting clinical prediction models that use regression or machine learning methods
Description: TRIPOD/TRIPOD+AI provides reporting guidance for studies that develop prediction models, scores, classifiers, biomarker signatures, or evaluate model performance in healthcare, regardless of whether regression or machine learning methods are used.
Aim: To improve complete, accurate, and transparent reporting of prediction model development and evaluation studies in healthcare, including studies using machine learning methods.

Scope.Coverage: Healthcare studies that develop a multivariable prediction model or evaluate its performance, including studies using regression and machine learning approaches.
Scope.Focus: Transparent reporting of context, data, participant flow, predictors, outcomes, analysis methods, model specification, model performance, fairness considerations, open science practices, and usability in care pathways.
Scope.ContrastWith: Not for randomized trial intervention reporting (CONSORT), observational association-only reporting (STROBE), diagnostic test accuracy reporting without a multivariable prediction-model/signature focus (STARD), or systematic review reporting (PRISMA). If the manuscript explicitly states that it was performed or reported according to TRIPOD and develops or validates a diagnostic/prognostic model, score, signature, classifier, or risk tool, TRIPOD+AI remains applicable alongside STARD when both guide types fit.

primary.description: Collins GS, Moons KGM, Dhiman P, et al. TRIPOD+AI statement: updated guidance for reporting clinical prediction models that use regression or machine learning methods. BMJ. 2024;385:e078378.

items.item_name: Identify prediction model study type, population, and outcome
items.item_question_background: Readers should immediately recognize whether the paper develops or evaluates a prediction model and for whom and what outcome.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract wording in the title that identifies development/evaluation, target population, and predicted outcome.

items.item_question_background: Abstracts often drive indexing and first-pass appraisal, so key model study details should be present.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract whether the abstract covers context, objectives, data, modelling approach, performance measures, and conclusions consistent with TRIPOD+AI for Abstracts.

items.item_question_background: Clinical context and rationale support judging relevance and novelty.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract diagnostic/prognostic context, rationale, and references to existing models.

items.item_name: Target population, intended purpose, intended users
items.item_question_background: Model purpose and users clarify where and by whom the model is intended to be used.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract target population, intended use in care pathway, and intended users (for example clinicians, patients, public).

items.item_question_background: Pre-existing inequalities can affect fairness and transportability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract statements about known inequalities between sociodemographic groups relevant to this prediction context.

items.item_question_background: Clear objectives prevent ambiguity about whether the work is development, evaluation, or both.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract explicit objectives and whether the study is development, evaluation, or both.

items.item_question_background: Data source and representativeness are central to bias and applicability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract development/evaluation data sources, rationale for using them, and representativeness claims.

items.item_name: Data collection dates and follow-up end
items.item_question_background: Study timing supports temporal context and possible dataset shift assessment.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract start/end dates for participant accrual and follow-up end date where applicable.

items.item_question_background: Setting details support transportability judgments.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract healthcare setting, number of centres, and centre locations.

items.item_question_background: Eligibility criteria define the modelled population.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract participant inclusion and exclusion criteria.

items.item_question_background: Treatment effects can influence predictor-outcome relations and model validity.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract treatment details and how treatments were handled in development/evaluation analyses.

items.item_question_background: Preprocessing decisions can materially alter model behaviour and fairness.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract preprocessing and quality-check steps and whether they were similar across relevant sociodemographic groups.

items.item_question_background: Outcome definition and timing determine target validity and comparability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract outcome definition, prediction horizon, how/when assessed, rationale for outcome choice, and consistency across sociodemographic groups.

items.item_question_background: Subjective outcome assessment can introduce systematic bias.
items.item_extraction_instruction: If subjective outcome interpretation is involved, extract assessor qualifications and demographic characteristics.

items.item_question_background: Blinding can reduce measurement and information bias.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract actions taken to blind outcome assessment.

items.item_question_background: Predictor selection strategy affects reproducibility and overfitting risk.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract how candidate predictors were chosen and any pre-selection before model building.

items.item_question_background: Predictor definitions must be explicit for replication and implementation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract all predictors, how/when measured, and any blinding of predictor assessment.

items.item_question_background: Subjective predictor measurement can introduce systematic bias.
items.item_extraction_instruction: If subjective predictor interpretation is involved, extract assessor qualifications and demographic characteristics.

items.item_question_background: Inadequate sample size can yield unstable and biased performance estimates.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract how development/evaluation sample sizes were determined and any calculation or adequacy justification.

items.item_question_background: Missing-data handling can substantially affect model estimates and performance.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract missing data methods and reasons for omitted data.

items.item_question_background: Improper data partitioning can lead to information leakage.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract how data were used for development and evaluation, including partitioning strategy and sample-size considerations.

items.item_question_background: Feature handling choices alter model behaviour and comparability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract predictor handling details such as transformations, scaling, and functional forms.

items.item_name: Model type, rationale, building steps, tuning, internal validation
items.item_question_background: Model-building transparency is required for reproducibility and appraisal.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract model type(s), rationale, model-building workflow, hyperparameter tuning, and internal validation approach.

items.item_question_background: Cluster-level heterogeneity can affect generalisability and calibration.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract whether and how heterogeneity across clusters/settings was handled and quantified.

items.item_question_background: Performance must be evaluated with transparent, suitable metrics and rationale.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract all performance measures/plots (for example discrimination, calibration, clinical utility) and rationale.

items.item_question_background: Model updating changes interpretation of evaluation results and transportability.
items.item_extraction_instruction: If evaluation included updating/recalibration, extract what was updated and for which settings or sociodemographic groups.

items.item_name: How predictions were calculated for evaluation
items.item_question_background: Transparent prediction-generation steps prevent hidden implementation discrepancies.
items.item_extraction_instruction: For evaluation studies, extract how predictions were computed (formula, code, model object, API, etc.).

items.item_question_background: Class imbalance handling can materially affect model calibration and discrimination.
items.item_extraction_instruction: If class imbalance methods were used, extract why/how they were used and any recalibration methods.

items.item_question_background: Fairness handling is central to equitable model performance.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract fairness-related approaches and their rationale.

items.item_question_background: Output format and thresholding determine clinical interpretation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract model output type and any classification threshold definitions and rationale.

items.item_name: Differences between development and evaluation data
items.item_question_background: Dataset differences can explain changes in model performance.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract differences between development and evaluation datasets in setting, eligibility, outcome, and predictors.

items.item_question_background: Ethics governance supports trustworthy and compliant model research.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract ethics committee/IRB name and consent or waiver details.

items.item_question_background: Funding transparency helps assess potential influences on methods and reporting.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract funding sources and explicit role of funders.

items.item_question_background: Conflict disclosure helps readers interpret potential bias sources.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract conflict-of-interest and financial disclosure statements for all authors.

items.item_question_background: Protocol transparency supports verification of prespecified methods.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract where the protocol can be accessed, or explicit statement that no protocol was prepared.

items.item_question_background: Registration supports transparency and discourages selective reporting.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract registry name and registration number, or explicit statement that the study was not registered.

items.item_question_background: Data availability statements support reproducibility and secondary assessment.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract availability and access conditions for study data.

items.item_question_background: Code availability is critical for reproducibility in ML and statistical pipelines.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract availability and access conditions for analytical code.

items.item_question_background: Patient/public involvement can improve relevance and acceptability of model research.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract how patients/public were involved in design, conduct, reporting, interpretation, or dissemination, or explicit no-involvement statement.

items.item_question_background: Participant flow clarifies potential selection effects and denominator changes.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract participant flow, counts with/without outcome, follow-up summary where relevant, and flow diagram mention.

items.item_question_background: Characteristics are needed to judge applicability, fairness, and missingness context.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract key dates, predictor demographics, treatments, sample size, number of outcome events, follow-up, missingness, and group differences.

items.item_question_background: Distribution shifts between datasets can explain performance changes.
items.item_extraction_instruction: For evaluation studies, extract comparison of key predictor/outcome distributions versus development data.

items.item_question_background: Analysis-level denominators are needed for reproducibility and risk-of-bias assessment.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract number of participants and outcome events for each analysis stage.

items.item_name: Full model specification and access constraints
items.item_question_background: Independent evaluation and implementation require full model specification.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract model formula/code/object/API details and any restrictions on access or reuse.

items.item_question_background: Performance with uncertainty and subgroup information is essential for interpretation.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract performance estimates with confidence intervals overall and in key subgroups, plus any supporting plots.

items.item_question_background: Cluster heterogeneity informs transportability and implementation risk.
items.item_extraction_instruction: If assessed, extract heterogeneity results for model performance across clusters/settings.

items.item_question_background: Updated model details are needed when evaluation triggers recalibration or revision.
items.item_extraction_instruction: For evaluation studies with updating, extract updated model details and subsequent performance.

items.item_question_background: Interpretation should reflect objectives, prior evidence, and fairness implications.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract overall interpretation with explicit reference to objectives, prior studies, and fairness considerations.

items.item_question_background: Explicit limitations are needed for honest appraisal of bias and uncertainty.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract discussed limitations (for example representativeness, sample size, overfitting, missing data) and their expected effects on bias, uncertainty, and generalisability.

items.item_name: Handling poor or unavailable input data
items.item_question_background: Operational guidance is needed for real-world deployment with imperfect inputs.
items.item_extraction_instruction: For development-focused studies, extract recommendations for assessing/handling poor or unavailable predictor inputs during implementation.

items.item_question_background: Users and required expertise affect safe and feasible model deployment.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract expected user interaction with model inputs/usage and required user expertise.

items.item_name: Future research for applicability and generalisability
items.item_question_background: Future-work plans should address applicability and transportability gaps.
items.item_extraction_instruction: Extract proposed next research steps, especially those targeting applicability and generalisability.

Aliases: Transparent Reporting of a multivariable prediction model for Individual Prognosis Or Diagnosis
EvidenceExamplesPositive: We developed and externally validated a prognostic prediction model for 30-day mortality and report calibration slope, c-statistic, and decision-curve analysis.
This study evaluates a machine-learning diagnostic prediction model in a multicentre cohort and reports discrimination and calibration with confidence intervals.
EvidenceExamplesNegative: This randomized trial compares two treatments and does not develop or evaluate a multivariable prediction model.
This paper is a systematic review of prediction studies and does not report primary model development or model performance evaluation.
Matching.Explain: This catalog item covers TRIPOD and TRIPOD+AI prediction-model reporting. Select it for healthcare manuscripts that develop or evaluate diagnostic/prognostic prediction models, scores, classifiers, or biomarker signatures. It can apply alongside STARD when a diagnostic-accuracy manuscript also develops or validates a multivariable prediction/signature model, especially when the manuscript explicitly says it was reported in accordance with TRIPOD.
